channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Lula detona ausência de Tarcísio de Freitas em lançamento de fábrica de trens em SP
item.description: President Lula criticized the absence of São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas at the inauguration of a Chinese train factory in Araraquara, emphasizing the 7 billion reais investment and its potential to generate jobs and modernity. He also stated that the 2026 elections will be a contest between 'truth' and 'lie'.

item.title: Boulos diz que há omissão de governadores no ICMS dos combustíveis
item.description: Guilherme Boulos, Secretary-General of the Presidency, accused governors of failing to reduce ICMS on fuels, particularly diesel, and blamed recent price hikes on the greed of distributors and gas stations, despite federal tax exemptions. He made these remarks after a meeting with truckers at the Planalto Palace, linking the situation to the trade war initiated by Donald Trump against Iran. The federal government announced it will expand oversight by ANP, PF, Senacon, and Procons to combat abusive practices in the fuel supply chain.

item.title: Lula diz lamentar ausência de Tarcísio em evento em Araraquara e diz que eleição de 2026 será disputa entre 'verdade e mentira'
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva regretted the absence of São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas at an event for the installation of a Chinese train factory in Araraquara, SP, noting his absence was felt during the ceremony. Lula stated that the 2026 election will be a contest between 'truth and lies,' underscoring the deepening political polarization in Brazil.

item.title: Advogado de Bolsonaro assume defesa do cunhado de Vorcaro
item.description: Fabiano Zettel, brother-in-law of Daniel Vorcaro and detained in Operation Compliance Zero, replaced his defense lawyers with Celso Vilardi, who also defends Jair Bolsonaro. The change suggests Zettel may accept a plea deal, similar to Vorcaro, whose potential agreement is highly anticipated in Brasília given his extensive ties across the three branches of government.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula inaugura novas áreas de hospital em São Carlos
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva inaugurates on Wednesday (March 25, 2026) the expansion of the Federal University of São Carlos University Hospital, adding new medical and surgical beds, operating rooms, and a Nephrology Unit, funded by the Novo PAC. The expansion aims to increase SUS healthcare capacity and strengthen health professional training.

item.title: Primeiro caça Gripen fabricado no Brasil é apresentado e país ingressa em seleto clube
item.description: Brazil unveiled its first domestically manufactured F-39E Gripen fighter jet, joining the select group of nations capable of producing supersonic combat aircraft. President Lula attended the ceremony without speaking, merely christening the aircraft, while the Defense Minister emphasized the contract for 36 jets, technology transfer, and maintenance autonomy. Launched in 2014, the program has so far spent R$ 16.75 billion and is now scheduled for completion by 2032, with 15 aircraft to be built at Embraer’s facility in Gavião Peixoto.

item.title: Boulos: caminhoneiros não podem pagar o preço de governadores não mexerem no ICMS
item.description: Minister of the General Secretariat of the Presidency, Guilherme Boulos, criticized governors for not reducing the ICMS on fuels, accusing them of omission amid rising diesel prices driven by artificial increases by distributors and the U.S.-Iran conflict. He said the government will maintain a permanent dialogue with truckers while the provisional measure on minimum freight rates is debated in Congress, strengthening fuel station inspections and ruling out strikes, as prior demands have already been met.

item.title: Eduardo Leite rejeita papel de vice em candidatura ao Planalto
item.description: Eduardo Leite, governor of Rio Grande do Sul and member of the PSD, rejected the possibility of running as vice president on a presidential ticket and stated he is working to make his own candidacy for the Planalto Palace viable in the 2026 elections. The comment was made on Wednesday, the 25th, during internal PSD discussions, emphasizing his intent to lead a majoritarian ticket.

item.title: Cortejado pelo PSB, Pacheco janta com João Campos nesta 4ª feira
item.description: Recife mayor and national PSB president João Campos will dine with Senator Rodrigo Pacheco (PSD-MG) in Brasília on Wednesday, March 25, 2026, to persuade Pacheco to join the PSB and run for governor of Minas Gerais, thereby securing a political base for President Lula in the state. Pacheco cannot remain in the PSD due to the allegiance of Vice Governor Mateus Simões (also PSD) to Governor Romeu Zema (Novo). The party-switching window without risk of mandate loss extends until April 3, 2026.

item.title: TSE determina cassação imediata de mandato de Rodrigo Bacellar, presidente da Alerj
item.description: The TSE ordered the immediate removal of Rodrigo Bacellar's mandate, suspended president of the Alerj, in a decision issued on Wednesday (25). The ruling impacts the leadership of the Rio de Janeiro State Legislative Assembly and underscores efforts to combat administrative misconduct. The case is linked to investigations into conduct incompatible with the exercise of parliamentary office.

item.title: A trajetória do último dos gigantes pela luta dos direitos civis nos EUA
item.description: The burial of Reverend Jesse Jackson marks the end of an era in the U.S. civil rights movement, recognized as the last of the giants who transformed Black political inclusion in America. His leadership inspired figures like Barack Obama and Kamala Harris, solidified diversity in political discourse, and encouraged civic engagement through voting and boycotts of discriminatory practices. In Brazil, he left a legacy by supporting the creation of Zumbi dos Palmares University and the Corporate Initiative for Racial Equality.

item.title: Oposição teme que Bolsonaro em casa interfira mais em campanha de Flávio e dificulte acordos
item.description: Opposition figures welcomed the decision by Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes to place former President Jair Bolsonaro under house arrest, but fear his continued presence at home could interfere more directly in the presidential campaign of Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, complicating political alliances. While seen as a blow to bolsonarism, the move raises concerns about increased behind-the-scenes influence from the former president in the 2026 election.

item.title: Leite: PSD terá de decidir se é partido que defende a anistia ou fala de um Brasil diferente
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite states that the choice of the PSD's presidential pre-candidate in 2026 will be decisive in determining whether the party defends amnesty or pursues a vision of a different Brazil. He emphasizes that this decision goes beyond a name and reflects the ideological identity the PSD will assume before the country. The statement comes amid internal debates over the party's direction in the face of national polarization.

item.title: ‘Burocrático, chato e enfadonho’: Enquanto direita avança, juventude francesa reduz participação nas eleições
item.description: French youth is increasingly disengaged from politics, perceiving it as bureaucratic and ineffective, contributing to a turnout of around 57% in the 2026 municipal elections — the second-highest abstention since 1958. While cities like Lyon, Paris, and Marseille re-elected left-wing mayors by narrow margins, the right advanced nationally, winning in Toulouse and securing 93 municipalities, signaling a conservative surge that contrasts with the political engagement seen in countries like Brazil.

item.title: CEO da Saab vê Brasil como polo regional de caças supersônicos
item.description: Saab CEO Micael Johansson stated that Brazil has the potential to become a regional hub for supersonic fighters, emphasizing the production of the F-39E Gripen by Embraer in Gavião Peixoto. He noted that this delivery is just the beginning, with local capabilities ensuring continuous evolution and operational excellence for decades. The event featured federal and state authorities along with Swedish representatives, marking a milestone for Latin America's defense industry.

item.title: Bolsonaro tem reclamado de dor no ombro direito e médicos indicam cirurgia
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro is hospitalized at DF Star Hospital in Brasília for bilateral pneumonia and has reported right shoulder pain, with medical specialists indicating a need for surgical intervention. However, the surgery is being postponed due to his ongoing recovery from pneumonia, and he is expected to be discharged this Friday (27) after completing his antibiotic course. Bolsonaro, sentenced to 27 years in prison for attempting a coup, is currently under house arrest granted by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes, under strict conditions including an electronic ankle monitor and bans on cell phones and social media.

item.title: PT vota a favor de empréstimo pedido por Tarcísio, e bolsonarista critica
item.description: The São Paulo Legislative Assembly (Alesp) approved a bill proposed by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas authorizing a R$15 billion loan, with support from PT deputies. Bolsonarist state deputy Gil Diniz (PL) criticized the vote, accusing PT members of acting as part of the governor’s governing base.

item.title: Em Ribeirão Preto, Zema descarta ser vice e mira segundo turno com campanha anti-PT
item.description: Romeu Zema (Novo), pre-candidate for the presidency in 2026, ruled out being a running mate and said he will maintain his pre-candidacy until the end, emphasizing his distinct proposals. He pledged active support for any right-wing candidate if he doesn’t reach the runoff and reiterated his anti-PT campaign, accusing the party of hindering Brazil’s development with outdated ideas. Zema noted he currently has 4% to 5% in voting intentions and is confident of growth as he travels across the country.

item.title: Lula x Flávio: o resultado de ‘forte peso simbólico’ na nova pesquisa AtlasIntel
item.description: The AtlasIntel poll shows Flávio Bolsonaro leading Lula in a hypothetical runoff with 47.6% to 46.6%, within the margin of error, indicating a technical tie. Flávio’s gain is attributed to a strong transfer of votes from Jair Bolsonaro, while Lula has lost about 6.4 points since December due to unfavorable national and international contexts. Despite the symbolic lead, analysts stress the race remains tight and undecided, with the politically disengaged middle electorate likely to determine the outcome.

item.title: [Coluna] Fazendo retrospectiva de um país complicado
item.description: A German columnist for DW Brasil ends her monthly column after eight years due to German government budget cuts. She reflects on her time in Brazil, noting the polarized reader engagement — especially around Bolsonaro — and observes that despite widespread consensus on parliamentary corruption, no meaningful change occurs.

item.title: Trump não 'humilhou' Lula na Casa Branca; áudio foi alterado por IA
item.description: A misleading video claims Donald Trump humiliated Lula at the White House with insults and tariff threats, but the audio was altered using artificial intelligence. HIVE Moderation analysis showed a 99.1% probability of AI-manipulated audio, and no official or journalistic records support the claim. The video uses real footage from a Jake Tapper newscast, but the Portuguese dubbing is fabricated.

item.title: Leite agora diz que não cogita sair ao Senado e mira Planalto
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD) stated he is not considering a Senate run and is focused on securing a presidential candidacy for 2026. He aims to offer an alternative to the political polarization between Lula and Bolsonaro, noting that many Brazilians are divided between fears of Lula remaining in power and Bolsonaro returning. Leite met with PSD president Gilberto Kassab to discuss the party's electoral strategy after Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior ruled out a presidential bid.

item.title: Eleições 2026: Renan Santos e a volta por cima do jovem de direita
item.description: The stigmatized 'young right-winger' of the 70s–90s has reemerged as a rebellious alternative amid voter fatigue with two decades of PT rule and recurring scandals. The Free Brazil Movement (MBL), led by figures like Kim Kataguiri and Renan Santos, channels this shift through a modern aesthetic and a defense of liberalism, long demonized in Brazilian discourse as synonymous with betrayal. Renan Santos, the Missão party’s presidential candidate, reached 4.6% in voter intention in the Atlas/Bloomberg poll — exceeding Geraldo Alckmin’s 2018 showing.

item.title: O que resta a Cláudio Castro após o TSE torná-lo inelegível
item.description: The TSE barred former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL) from running for office until 2030 due to abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 campaign, following his resignation. The case centered on an alleged scheme of irregular hirings at the Ceperj Foundation, where roughly 27,000 temporary workers were allegedly deployed as electoral foot soldiers. Castro’s defense may still pursue clarifying appeals, regimental remedies, or a petition to the Supreme Federal Court.

item.title: CPI do INSS deve terminar 2ª fase sem conseguir avanços e com possibilidade de não ser prorrogada
item.description: The INSS CPI is expected to end its second phase without significant progress, with a risk of not being extended due to the possible overturning of Minister André Mendonça's decision by the STF. The opposition hoped the CPI would act as a 'steamroller' against President Lula, but the government, holding a majority in the committee, is likely to block the critical report by Deputy Alfredo Gaspar. Despite canceled sessions and few testimonies, CPI President Senator Carlos Viana is working to secure a consensus report to avoid a final failure.

item.title: Brasil está dividido entre medo de Lula e de Bolsonaro, diz Leite
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD) says Brazil is divided between fear of Lula staying in power and fear of Bolsonaro returning, arguing the PSD can offer a centrist alternative in the 2026 elections. He affirms his commitment to becoming the PSD's presidential candidate, rejecting smaller roles like Senate or vice-president, and states he will only leave his state governorship if national political conditions are favorable. Leite also notes internal competition with Ronaldo Caiado (União Brasil) and awaits direction following a meeting with PSD President Gilberto Kassab.

item.title: Plenário confirma mandatos de prefeita e vice-prefeita de Sertânia (PE)
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) unanimously confirmed the mandates of elected mayor Pollyanna Barbosa de Abreu and vice-mayor Teresa Raquel Rufino de Siqueira Viana of Sertânia (PE) in the 2024 elections, upholding the regional court’s decision. The courts rejected an Electoral Judicial Investigation Action (Aije) alleging economic power abuse through distribution of gifts, free services, machinery use, and local radio advertising, finding no proof of illicit intent or material impact on the election’s legitimacy. All cited services were documented via invoices, receipts, and payment records, negating claims of gratuitousness or unlawful purpose.

item.title: Eduardo Leite descarta ser vice e insiste em candidatura à presidência de 3ª via: 'brasileiro precisa de alternativa ao medo'
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD) ruled out running for vice-president or Senate and reiterated his commitment to leading a third-way presidential candidacy in 2026, framing it as an 'alternative to fear' amid polarization between Lula and Bolsonaro. He said he will seek support from PSD national president Gilberto Kassab to consolidate his electoral project, following Ratinho Júnior's withdrawal from the party's nomination race.

item.title: STF inicia julgamento de decisões sobre penduricalhos; entenda o que está em jogo
item.description: The STF begins ruling on decisions by ministers Flávio Dino and Gilmar Mendes that suspended 'penduricalhos' — extra payments above the constitutional salary cap (BRL 46,000) — for public servants and judges. Dino argued only indemnity payments explicitly defined in national law can exceed the cap, condemning Congress’s failure to legislate since EC 135/2024. Mendes extended the suspension to the Judiciary and MP, barring reliance on state laws and limiting CNJ/CNMP regulatory power.

item.title: Lei facilita crédito à exportação para micro e pequenas empresas
item.description: Law 15.359 establishes the Brazilian System of Official Support for Export Credit, easing access for micro, small, and medium enterprises to international markets via a single online portal, expanded credit insurance coverage, and inclusion of green and high-tech projects focused on decarbonization and resource efficiency. The law mandates transparency for BNDES export credit operations, bans new loans to delinquent foreign entities, and limits personal liability of public officials to cases of gross negligence or intent. President Lula vetoed provisions requiring the Union to cover FGCE losses and act as guarantor for private foreign trade operations, citing fiscal risk and absence of budgetary allocation.

item.title: Lula batiza primeiro caça Gripen produzido no Brasil
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva attended the christening ceremony of the first F-39E Gripen fighter jet produced in Brazil, held at Embraer’s airfield in Gavião Peixoto, São Paulo. The aircraft, developed through a partnership between Embraer and Saab, marks a milestone for national air sovereignty and the defense industrial base, having already generated over 2,000 direct and 10,000 indirect jobs. Lula also viewed the eVTOL flying car prototype from Eve Air Mobility, an Embraer subsidiary, during the event.

item.title: Crusoé: Proibir manifestações é mais um ato de censura de Moraes
item.description: The decision by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes to place former President Jair Bolsonaro under 90 days of house arrest, including a ban on accessing or participating in any camps, demonstrations, or gatherings, is criticized as another act of censorship against Brazilians. The Crusoé article, published on O Antagonista, frames the measure as part of a broader pattern of restricting freedom of assembly and expression.

item.title: O Legislativo se vende porque a elite econômica o compra
item.description: The text criticizes the National Congress for being compromised by the economic elite, suggesting that legislators are effectively 'for sale' due to the influence of economic power over parliamentary decisions. This dynamic undermines democratic representation and prioritizes policies that benefit a few at the expense of the public good. Maria Inês Nassif, a journalist and former advisor to the Lula Institute, identifies this relationship as structural in Brazilian politics.

item.title: Planalto aponta um dos motivos para o aumento da desaprovação de Lula
item.description: The rise in disapproval of Lula's government, reaching 53.5% according to an AtlasIntel poll, is being attributed by the Planalto to increased fuel prices between March 18 and 23. The decline was most pronounced among 16-24 year olds (rising from 58.6% to 72.7%) and those aged 60+ (from 39.2% to 50.8%), with disapproval especially high among evangelicals (85.5%) and in the Central-West region (65.9%).

item.title: Lula edita MP com mais R$ 15 bi para programa de ajuda a empresas afetadas pela guerra
item.description: President Lula issued Provisional Measure 1.345, allocating an additional R$15 billion to the Brazil Sovereign program to support micro, small, and medium enterprises affected by the Middle East war, using unused funds from the FGE and other budgetary sources. He also signed Law 15.359, establishing the Brazilian Official Credit System for Export, which modernizes export financing and insurance, incorporates BNDES rules restricting loans to defaulting countries, and promotes green and decarbonization operations. The measures aim to preserve jobs, ensure competitiveness, and enhance transparency via a unified portal and annual reports to Congress.

item.title: Cassação de Cláudio Castro pressiona Cármen Lúcia a pautar caso de Antonio Denarium
item.description: The cassation of Rio de Janeiro's governor, Cláudio Castro, has increased pressure on TSE President Minister Cármen Lúcia to schedule the case of Antonio Denarium, governor of Roraima, whose process has been stalled for two years. Denarium and his running mate have already been removed from office four times by the Roraima TRE for political and economic abuse of power, and two TSE ministers have already voted in favor of cassation. Although Denarium resigned on March 27 to run for Senate, a TSE ruling could still confirm his ineligibility, preventing him from running.

item.title: TSE mantém desaprovação de contas de vereador em Bom Jesus do Itabapoana (RJ)
item.description: The TSE upheld the rejection of campaign accounts for elected councilman Marcelo Vieira Pereira (PP) of Bom Jesus do Itabapoana (RJ) for the 2024 elections, citing irregularities in the use of Electoral Fund resources. The Court confirmed the fine and the obligation to return funds to the National Treasury, following the TRE-RJ’s identification of serious flaws in financial reporting, including indirect transfers from a mayoral candidate of another party.

item.title: Tarcísio propõe Ibirapuera com lojas subterrâneas e ar-condicionado
item.description: Tarcísio de Freitas proposes the concession of the Constâncio Vaz Guimarães Sports Complex, including Ibirapuera Gymnasium, with lowered flooring, increased capacity, and an underground commercial center with air conditioning. The project, open for public consultation until April 24, also includes the Mario Covas Olympic Village and respects the Iphan heritage listing, excluding stadium renovation requirements as the Ícaro track is already being rebuilt by COHAB.

item.title: Lula lança o 1º caça supersônico produzido no Brasil
item.description: President Lula attended the unveiling of the F-39E Gripen, Brazil's first domestically produced supersonic fighter jet, developed in partnership with Saab. The aircraft represents a technological leap for national defense, with 36 units planned for acquisition and local production aimed at strengthening Brazil's industrial autonomy. The event also featured Embraer's electric vertical takeoff and landing vehicle prototype, dubbed the 'flying car'.

item.title: Crusoé: Lula perde mais uma com Michelle Bachelet
item.description: The President of Chile, José Antonio Kast, withdrew Chile's support for Michelle Bachelet's candidacy for UN Secretary-General, a bid previously backed by the Lula administration last year. This decision marks another diplomatic setback for Brazilian President Lula, who is increasingly isolated in Latin America.

item.title: TSE condena Cláudio Castro, mas ex-governador vai disputar Senado 'sub judice'
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL) was condemned by the TSE and would become ineligible, but he plans to appeal and run for Senate 'sub judice', keeping his candidacy active while appeals are pending. His potential replacement in the PL ticket by delegate Felipe Curi, former Rio de Janeiro Civil Police Secretary, is being discussed by allies of Flávio Bolsonaro, who see greater electoral viability in Curi following Castro’s conviction.

item.title: Petrobras diz analisar possibilidade de recomprar a refinaria de Mataripe
item.description: Petrobras stated it is analyzing the possibility of repurchasing the Mataripe refinery in Bahia, which was sold to Acelen during the Bolsonaro government. The statement came in response to a CVM request following President Lula’s remark that 'we will buy' the refinery, acknowledging it may take time. The company noted it had previously informed the market about evaluating investment opportunities in this asset.

item.title: ‘Governei o Rio de Janeiro dentro da legalidade’, diz Castro antes de recorrer ao TSE
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL) criticized the TSE's ineligibility ruling until 2030, claiming he governed within the law and responsibly. He was convicted of abuse of political and economic power for hiring over 27,000 temporary workers at Fundação Ceperj to act as electoral operatives in the 2022 elections, though he denies the hires influenced his 4.9 million-vote victory. Despite being ineligible, the PL maintains his Senate candidacy sub judice, hoping to overturn the decision via a favorable STF ruling.

item.title: Entenda o PL Antifacção sancionado por Lula
item.description: President Lula signed the Anti-Gang Bill, establishing a legal framework to combat organized crime in Brazil by increasing penalties and facilitating asset seizures. He vetoed provisions equating penalties for members and non-members of gangs and the allocation of seized assets to state funds, citing unconstitutionality and the need to protect social movements.

item.title: Os papéis que Michelle e Carlos devem assumir no PL com a prisão domiciliar de Bolsonaro
item.description: With Jair Bolsonaro under house arrest, Carlos Bolsonaro (PL-SC) is expected to serve as the main liaison between the former president and the party, while Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) focuses on his presidential pre-candidacy and Michelle Bolsonaro returns to political activity by leading PL Mulher to revitalize the party's women's wing and strengthen the family's influence in regional races.

item.title: Candidatura seguirá mesmo com Bolsonaro elegível, diz Flávio
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) stated he will be the presidential candidate for his political group, even if former president Jair Bolsonaro (PL) becomes eligible, revealing that his father designated him for the role. The former president's defense plans to file actions to overturn his STF conviction, citing alleged procedural nullities. Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to 27 years and 3 months for attempted coup but is serving 90 days of house arrest for health reasons, a measure Flávio criticized as 'exotic'.

item.title: Dólar cai com petróleo, apesar de solução para guerra parecer distante
item.description: The U.S. dollar declines in the spot market amid falling oil prices and signals of a possible one-month ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran, though hostilities between Israel and Iran persist and Iranian officials deny direct negotiations. In Brazil, the Consumer Confidence Index rose in March, while an Atlas/Bloomberg poll shows a technical tie between Flávio Bolsonaro and Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva in a potential second-round runoff. The Senate passed the ReData bill to adjust tax rules for data centers, and Grupo Fictor’s CEO is under investigation by the Federal Police for alleged fraud against Caixa involving shell companies and money laundering.

item.title: Ao vivo: Lula participa de apresentação do F-39E Gripen
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva attends the presentation of the F-39E Gripen fighter jet, the first supersonic aircraft produced in Brazil, at an event in Embraer's Gavião Peixoto airfield (SP). The government stated the achievement places Brazil among a select group of nations capable of developing and producing high-complexity combat aircraft, a first in Latin America. Lula will also visit the 5 km Embraer runway and watch a demonstration of the Eve Air Mobility eVTOL, an electric vertical takeoff and landing vehicle for urban air mobility.

item.title: Evento milionário do governo com pequenos agricultores vira palanque eleitoral para Lula
item.description: The Lula government held a R$14 million event with small farmers in Brasília, which served as an electoral platform. Lula delivered a polarizing speech, accusing the opposition of seeking to destroy social gains and warning about 'foxes in the henhouse'. Minister Paulo Teixeira, who resigned to run for office, was honored.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro abre vantagem numérica sobre Lula no 2º turno, dentro da margem de erro, diz pesquisa
item.description: The March AtlasIntel/Bloomberg poll shows Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) leading Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) 47.6% to 46.6% in a hypothetical 2026 presidential runoff, within the 1-point margin of error. Bolsonaro gained one point since February, while Lula remained flat, signaling a marginal shift in his favor amid converging trends. The trend reflects growing pressure on Lulu from INSS pension investigations, billion-dollar bank fraud allegations, Carnival backlash, and seasonal economic stressors, contributing to a 'low tide' in his popularity.
